title: A Real Pain
logline: Two mismatched cousins travel to Poland on a Holocaust tour to honor their deceased grandmother, confronting their relationship and family history.
protagonist: David and Benji Kaplan (dual protagonists - cousins)
antagonistOrConflict: The painful dynamics of their relationship, grief over their grandmother's death, and confronting family Holocaust history
tone: Bittersweet, melancholic, with moments of dark humor and emotional intimacy

actBreaks.description: The cousins arrive at their Warsaw hotel after the first day of touring, establishing their dynamic and the tour group.

validation.ruling: Scene #34 shows the group entering the Warsaw hotel after their day of sightseeing, with David feeling left out of the group dynamic. This marks the end of the setup phase and David's growing awareness of his disconnect from both Benji and the tour experience.

actBreaks.description: The group returns from visiting Majdanek concentration camp, marking the emotional climax of their Holocaust education.

validation.ruling: Scene #94 in the van after visiting Majdanek shows Benji openly crying while David watches heartbroken, representing the emotional devastation that transforms both characters. This moment fundamentally changes the story's direction as they process the weight of their heritage and trauma together.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This is a character-driven journey film that follows the classic three-act structure while maintaining an intimate, observational tone. The first act establishes the cousins' contrasting personalities through their travel to Poland and introduction to the tour group. The second act deepens their exploration of both their family history and personal tensions, culminating in the visit to Majdanek. The third act focuses on their final bonding and separation, ending with a return to the opening airport imagery. The structure mirrors the physical journey from New York to Poland and back, but the emotional journey moves from avoidance to confrontation to acceptance.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing shifts dramatically between rapid-fire dialogue scenes (like the extended restaurant conversation in scene 70) and contemplative, slower moments (the rooftop scene 104-105). The middle section maintains steady momentum through the tour activities, while the final act deliberately slows down for the emotional climax and resolution.

incitingIncident.description: David arrives at JFK still leaving messages when Benji surprises him from behind, and they reunite with warm hugs and playful banter.

validation.ruling: Scene #5 establishes the central relationship and journey when David and Benji reunite at JFK Airport before their Poland trip. This moment launches the main narrative conflict by bringing together these two cousins with unresolved tension, forcing David into a situation he cannot avoid and setting up the emotional stakes that will drive the entire story.

midpoint.description: At the Jewish cemetery, James discusses the history while Benji grows visibly frustrated with James's constant talking, leading to nonverbal communication between the cousins and Benji approaching James and Eloge.

validation.ruling: Scene #68 at the Jewish cemetery serves as the midpoint where Benji shifts from passive participation to actively confronting James about treating the dead as 'factoids' rather than real people. This eruption marks Benji's transition from reactive to proactive behavior, significantly raising the personal stakes and revealing his deeper emotional wounds that will drive the second half of the story.

climax.description: While sharing a joint on the rooftop, David and Benji spot the Majdanek concentration camp in the distance, then David asks about Benji's plans for returning to Binghamton, making Benji uncomfortable.

validation.ruling: Scene #105 on the rooftop represents the emotional climax where the cousins share an intimate moment overlooking Majdanek concentration camp, leading to a crucial conversation about Benji's future that exposes the deep gulf between their life paths. This scene resolves the main emotional question about their relationship while demonstrating both characters' growth through their ability to have this vulnerable exchange.

scenes.heading: INT. JFK AIRPORT - MORNING
scenes.summary: Benji Kaplan sits alone on an airport bench with a melancholy expression as credits roll and the title appears.

scenes.heading: EXT. BROOKLYN STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: David hurries down a Brooklyn street while leaving a voicemail for Benji about meeting at the airport.

scenes.heading: EXT. BQE - DAY
scenes.summary: David's Uber sits stuck in traffic on the BQE.

scenes.heading: INT. UBER - DAY
scenes.summary: David frantically leaves multiple voicemails for Benji from the backseat of his Uber.

scenes.heading: INT. JFK AIRPORT - DAY
scenes.summary: David arrives at JFK still leaving messages when Benji surprises him from behind, and they reunite with warm hugs and playful banter.
scenes.turningPointNote: The cousins finally meet, establishing their dynamic and launching the story forward

scenes.heading: INT. JFK AIRPO RT HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: The cousins walk toward security as David subtly throws away his yogurt.

scenes.heading: INT. JFK AIRPORT SECURITY — DAY
scenes.summary: Benji charms a TSA agent named Jasmine while going through security as David watches nervously then admiringly.

scenes.heading: INT. JFK AIRPORT GATE - DAY
scenes.summary: At the gate, Benji claims he's been 'great' when David asks how he's been, creating unspoken tension as David wants to discuss 'the last few months' but can't.

scenes.heading: INT. AIRPLANE - DAY
scenes.summary: The cousins board the plane and Benji asks David to take the middle seat.

scenes.heading: INT. AIRPLANE - DAY
scenes.summary: David sits cramped in the middle seat while Benji spreads out comfortably, and Benji dismisses David's digital ad sales job as something 'everybody hates.'

scenes.heading: INT. AIRPLANE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: David stays awake watching videos of his son Abe while Benji sleeps with empty wine bottles on his tray.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW AIRPORT - EARLY MORNING
scenes.summary: The exhausted cousins walk through Warsaw airport as David suggests breakfast but Benji shakes his head with a dark look.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW AIRPORT CUSTOMS AREA - MORNING
scenes.summary: David panics silently as they go through customs while Benji remains unfazed.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW AIRPORT - MORNING
scenes.summary: Benji looks bleak but suddenly brightens when he spots their Heritage Tours driver.

scenes.heading: INT. CAR - MORNING
scenes.summary: In the car, David watches videos of his son while Benji stares out the window, and Benji asks to see the video of Abe.

scenes.heading: EXT. WARSAW - MORNING
scenes.summary: They arrive at the Warsaw Central Hotel.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL - MORNING
scenes.summary: At check-in, Benji receives a mysterious package that has been waiting for days, baffling David.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: Walking to their room, Benji throws his arm around David's neck and expresses happiness about being there together.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: In their hotel room, Benji compliments David's feet and compares them to their grandmother's, sparking a memory about her pink plastic sandals.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL HALLWAY - MORNING
scenes.summary: The cousins walk down the hallway with Benji freshly showered and David sweaty and exhausted.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW LOBBY - MORNIN G
scenes.summary: James, their British tour guide, intercepts them in the hotel lobby.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL RESTAURANT - DAY
scenes.summary: James introduces the cousins to their tour group and gives a lengthy introduction about himself and the tour, while Benji immediately charms everyone and David feels overwhelmed by the group dynamics.
scenes.turningPointNote: Introduction of the tour group and establishment of group dynamics that will drive much of the story

scenes.heading: EXT. WARSAW - MONTAGE
scenes.summary: A montage of Warsaw buildings and streets set to Chopin's racing Etude.

scenes.heading: EXT. WARSAW GHETTO UPRISING MONUMENT - DAY
scenes.summary: James gathers the group at the Warsaw Ghetto Uprising Monument and explains its significance in dispelling myths about Jewish resistance.

scenes.heading: EXT. WARSAW STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: The group walks along a street in front of Soviet-era apartment buildings.

scenes.heading: EXT. WARSAW STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: Benji buys water for the group but realizes he has no money, so David gives him złotys to pay.

scenes.heading: EXT. KRASINSKI GARDEN - DAY
scenes.summary: Walking through Krasinski Garden, Benji muses about how they might have lived in Poland if not for the war while David seems less engaged with the philosophical speculation.

scenes.heading: EXT. WALICOW STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: The group walks through Warsaw's ruins that abut modern Central European architecture.

scenes.heading: EXT. PLAC GRZYBOWSKI - D AY
scenes.summary: Benji playfully skips along stones jutting from a pond while the group waits patiently for him to finish.

scenes.heading: EXT. PLAC GRZYBOWSKI CAFE - DAY
scenes.summary: David sits alone at a cafe until Benji joins him, and Benji reveals intimate details about Marcia's recent divorce and dating life that she confided in him.

scenes.heading: EXT. KREDYTOWA STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: The group walks through a bohemian street from a high vantage point.

scenes.heading: EXT. Ś WI Ę TOJERSKA STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: The group passes a fragment of the old Ghetto Wall.

scenes.heading: EXT. WARSAW UPRISING MONUMENT - DAY
scenes.summary: At the Warsaw Uprising Monument, James explains Polish history while Benji enthusiastically poses for photos with the bronze soldiers, embarrassing David who reluctantly participates.

scenes.heading: EXT. WARSAW HOTEL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The group enters the hotel with good energy from the day while David feels left out.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL ELEVATOR — NIGHT
scenes.summary: In the elevator, Benji warmly says goodnight to the other tour members as he and David get off at their floor.

scenes.heading: EXT. WARSAW HOTEL HOTEL HALLWAY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The cousins walk down the hotel hallway toward their room.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Benji asks David to come into the bathroom with him.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL BATHROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: In the cramped bathroom, Benji intensely thanks David for the trip and acknowledges David's discomfort with groups, while David denies being uncomfortable socially.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Benji tries to open the hotel window to smoke but it only opens a few inches, frustrating him as David wants to sleep and Benji insists on staying up together.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL ELEVATOR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: David and Benji ride the elevator with Benji carrying a blunt and a fun spirit between them.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL HALLWAY OF TOP FLOOR — NIGHT
scenes.summary: The cousins find a door to the roof but David worries about the alarm while Benji wants to risk it.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL STAIRWELL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The boys giggle as they race up the stairwell and Benji opens the roof door.

scenes.heading: EXT. WARSAW HOTEL ROOF — NIGHT
scenes.summary: On the hotel roof overlooking Warsaw, the cousins share a joint and Benji tells David he's 'an awesome guy stuck inside the body of someone who's always running late' while reminiscing about their past adventures.
scenes.turningPointNote: Key bonding moment that reveals the core of their relationship dynamic and shared history

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL ELEVATOR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The cousins ride the elevator back down in silence.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The stoned cousins lie on their beds eating chips and watching TV, with Benji apologizing for calling David a lightweight and David apologizing for always falling asleep during their past adventures.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL ROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: David wakes up to find Benji still sleeping surrounded by empty mini vodka bottles.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL BATHROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: David FaceTimes with Priya in the bathroom, showers, and takes a pill.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL ROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: David tries to wake the sleeping Benji, who has a pained expression on his face and groans when roused.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL HALLWAY - MORNING
scenes.summary: Benji reluctantly follows David through the hotel hallway.

scenes.heading: EXT. WARSAW HOTEL - MORNING
scenes.summary: The group waits outside as David apologizes for being late, while Benji suddenly perks up and charms everyone after claiming David woke him up two minutes ago.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW TRAIN STATION - DAY
scenes.summary: The group walks through Warsaw train station while Benji takes it all in like a little kid.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW TRAIN STATION PLATFORM - DAY
scenes.summary: James leads the group to board the train, announcing they have first class seats, while David looks happy and Benji looks skeptical.

scenes.heading: EXT. TRAIN - DAY
scenes.summary: The train moves southeast through Central Poland's countryside.

scenes.heading: INT. TRAIN - DAY
scenes.summary: Benji interrupts James's tour narration to suggest they move train cars because he feels uncomfortable being Jews in first class on a Polish train, creating an awkward moment with the group.

scenes.heading: INT. TRAIN CAR - DAY
scenes.summary: David walks through a crowded part of the train looking for Benji but doesn't see him.

scenes.heading: INT. SECOND TRAIN CAR - DAY
scenes.summary: David finds Benji sitting alone in another train car and joins him, with Benji expressing sadness that everyone just wants to have a joyride while David tries to comfort him.

scenes.heading: EXT. POLAND COUNTRYSIDE - DAY
scenes.summary: The train speeds past various locations in the Polish countryside.

scenes.heading: INT. TRAIN - DAY
scenes.summary: David wakes up from sleep to find Benji watching him, and they prepare to exit as the train pulls into a station.

scenes.heading: EXT. KRASNIK PLATFORM - DAY
scenes.summary: The boys discover they're at the wrong station (Krasnik instead of Lublin) because Benji didn't wake David when they passed their stop, leading to David's frustration with Benji's priorities.

scenes.heading: INT. TRAIN - DAY
scenes.summary: Benji explains his plan to avoid the ticket conductor on their way back to Lublin while philosophizing about the corporatization of travel and class inequality.

scenes.heading: INT. FRONT CAR - DAY
scenes.summary: The boys successfully reach the front car and sit in first class seats, with Benji deciding they've earned it this time.

scenes.heading: EXT. LUBLIN STATION — DAY
scenes.summary: The train arrives at Lublin station and the boys walk along the platform looking for the exit.

scenes.heading: EXT. LUBLIN STATION - DAY
scenes.summary: The boys emerge from the station to find James and the group waiting in various stages of irritation, with Benji blaming their lateness on David falling asleep.

scenes.heading: EXT. STAIRS TO CASTLE - DAY
scenes.summary: James guides the group up the castle stairs while explaining Lublin's rich Jewish history as 'The Jewish Oxford' and setting expectations for an enlightening day.

scenes.heading: EXT. BRIDGE FROM ZAMKOWA STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: David questions Eloge about his religious practices while Marcia approaches Benji to discuss his train comments about understanding suffering, leading to her sharing concerns about her wealthy daughter.

scenes.heading: EXT. LUBLIN - MONTAGE
scenes.summary: A montage shows various hidden remnants of Jewish life throughout Lublin while James narrates about memories frozen in time.

scenes.heading: EXT. GRAVEL STREET - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: The group walks along an uneven gravel path while Benji throws his arm around David and pinches his cheek.

scenes.heading: EXT. CEMETERY – DUSK
scenes.summary: At the Jewish cemetery, James discusses the history while Benji grows visibly frustrated with James's constant talking, leading to nonverbal communication between the cousins and Benji approaching James and Eloge.
scenes.turningPointNote: Benji's mounting frustration with James's lecturing style creates visible tension and sets up conflict

scenes.heading: EXT. RESTAURANT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Establishing shot of a lively restaurant in old town Lublin.

scenes.heading: INT. RESTAURANT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: At dinner, Benji calls the restaurant's Hava Nagila music antisemitic while Marcia tells a story about her uncle, leading to Benji eventually performing his own music and creating an uncomfortable scene.
scenes.turningPointNote: Benji's outburst and performance creates a major disruption and embarrassment for David

scenes.heading: EXT. RESTAURANT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: David emerges from the restaurant alone while Benji's music continues playing inside, becoming more grating as David walks back to the hotel.

scenes.heading: INT. LUBLIN HOTEL ENTRANCE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: David enters the Lublin hotel while a bastardized version of Benji's music continues in his head.

scenes.heading: INT. LUBLIN HOTEL ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: David takes a pill that gets stuck in his throat, tries unsuccessfully to FaceTime Priya, and lies in bed looking at his bare feet while alone in the room.

scenes.heading: INT. LUBLIN HOTEL ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: David wakes up at 2:40 AM to hear Benji enter the room, rifle through stuff, and leave again, leaving David confused and concerned.

scenes.heading: INT. LUBLIN HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: David peeks into the empty hallway looking for Benji.

scenes.heading: INT. LUBLIN HOTEL ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: David hurriedly gets dressed, irritated by Benji's disappearance.

scenes.heading: INT. LUBLIN HALLWAY - MIDDLE OF THE NIGHT
scenes.summary: David walks through the hotel hallway searching for Benji.

scenes.heading: INT. LUBLIN LOBBY - MIDDLE OF THE NIGHT
scenes.summary: David speaks with hotel front desk staff who haven't seen Benji.

scenes.heading: INT. LUBLIN HOTEL ROOM - MIDDLE OF THE NIGHT
scenes.summary: David lies in bed, angry and worried about Benji's whereabouts.

scenes.heading: INT. LUBLIN HOTEL ROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: David is startled awake by a phone call from Benji, who tells him everyone is waiting downstairs.

scenes.heading: INT. LUBLIN HOTEL ELEVATOR – MORNING
scenes.summary: David rides the elevator down to the lobby, visibly angry.

scenes.heading: INT. LUBLIN LOBBY — MORNING
scenes.summary: David confronts Benji about disappearing all night while Benji casually explains he was with Marcia and didn't want to wake David.

scenes.heading: INT. VAN - DAY
scenes.summary: James addresses the group about the upcoming taxing visit to a concentration camp while David and Benji sit separately in the van, and the massive camp comes into view.
scenes.turningPointNote: The arrival at the concentration camp marks a major tonal and emotional shift in the story

scenes.heading: EXT. MAJDANEK - DAY
scenes.summary: The bus arrives at the eerily sparse Majdanek concentration camp parking lot, a preserved camp without museum-style adornments.

scenes.heading: EXT. PARKING LOT - DAY
scenes.summary: James explains that Majdanek was only two miles from bustling Lublin as the group takes in the scope of the unnerving place and walks toward the entrance.

scenes.heading: EXT. ENTRANCE AREA - DAY
scenes.summary: James explains how the Nazis left Majdanek well-preserved in their haste as Benji and David look around, cowed by the enormity of the place.

scenes.heading: INT. BARRACKS - DAY
scenes.summary: The group enters the barracks as James explains they were built for 250 people but often housed 500 to 1000, and he reads from a survivor's account.

scenes.heading: INT. UNDRESSING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: The group stands in the bare windowless undressing room as James identifies the location.

scenes.heading: INT. SHOWER ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: The group enters the dank, low-ceiling shower room as James reads another harrowing personal account.

scenes.heading: INT. GAS CHAMBER - DAY
scenes.summary: In the gas chamber, James points out blue stains on the walls as Zyklon B residue while the group looks around in various stages of nausea and Benji unconsciously puts his hand on David's shoulder.

scenes.heading: INT. CREMATORIUM – DAY
scenes.summary: The group silently observes the completely preserved body-sized crematorium ovens while James says nothing.

scenes.heading: INT. SHOE DISPLAY BARRACK - DAY
scenes.summary: Benji and David stand together staring at an overwhelming mound of discarded victims' shoes displayed in the barrack.

scenes.heading: EXT. MAUSOLEUM - DAY
scenes.summary: The group arrives at the enormous spaceship-like Mausoleum, standing dwarfed beneath the massive structure.

scenes.heading: INT. VAN - DAY
scenes.summary: The group rides back to the hotel in various stages of shell-shock, with Benji openly crying while staring out the window as David watches heartbroken.
scenes.turningPointNote: The emotional impact of the concentration camp visit creates a profound shift, with Benji breaking down and David deeply affected

scenes.heading: EXT. LUBLIN HOTEL - DAY
scenes.summary: The group walks toward the hotel entrance after their concentration camp visit.

scenes.heading: INT. LUBLIN HOTEL - DAY
scenes.summary: James gathers the group in the lobby to discuss their feelings about the camp visit, with members expressing nausea and distress before announcing that Benji and David are leaving the tour.
scenes.turningPointNote: The cousins' departure from the tour group marks a significant structural shift toward their private journey

scenes.heading: EXT. LUBLIN - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: The cousins walk around Lublin with a nice thawing between them, strolling above the Czechówka River mural.

scenes.heading: EXT. OUTDOOR MARKET - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: At a makeshift bazaar, Benji tries on a Polish folk hat, David takes a picture, and despite David questioning the purchase, Benji buys and wears the hat.

scenes.heading: EXT. HILL OVERLOOKING CITY - DUSK
scenes.summary: The cousins eat greasy shawarma and trudge up a hill as the sun sets behind them.

scenes.heading: EXT. BREWERY STREET - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The cousins walk under illuminated flowers and butterflies while Benji playfully jumps to swat a butterfly, missing completely as David laughs.

scenes.heading: EXT. LUBLIN STREET OFFICE BUILDING - NIGHT
scenes.summary: David and Benji stand outside a tall office building as Benji reveals he has one last joint and they watch a smoker exit the building.

scenes.heading: INT. LUBLIN BUILDING LOBBY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The cousins follow the smoker into the building lobby, awkwardly trying to act casual as they pass an indifferent security guard.

scenes.heading: INT. ELEVATOR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: In the elevator with the smoker, Benji and David ride to the top floor in awkward silence until the smoker speaks in Polish and Benji responds with 'Cool.'

scenes.heading: EXT. ROOF - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The boys struggle with a rusty industrial door on a quiet rooftop before it pops open and they emerge.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROOF – NIGHT
scenes.summary: While sharing a joint on the rooftop, David and Benji spot the Majdanek concentration camp in the distance, then David asks about Benji's plans for returning to Binghamton, making Benji uncomfortable.
scenes.turningPointNote: The visual confrontation with the concentration camp and David's probing question about Benji's future create a moment of reckoning that shifts their dynamic

scenes.heading: INT. LUBLIN HOTEL HALLWAY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The boys walk back to their hotel room in slow motion.

scenes.heading: INT. LUBLIN HOTEL ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: David and Benji enter their hotel room silently, take turns showering with minimal interaction, and go to sleep in separate beds.

scenes.heading: INT. LUBLIN HOTEL ROOM – MORNING
scenes.summary: The next morning, David and Benji pack their belongings without speaking to each other.

scenes.heading: INT. LOBBY DINING AREA - DAY
scenes.summary: The cousins eat breakfast in silence until David's phone buzzes and he announces their taxi has arrived.

scenes.heading: INT. LUBLIN HOTEL - DAY
scenes.summary: David and Benji load their bags into a colorful taxi outside the hotel lobby.

scenes.heading: EXT. COUNTRYSIDE - DAY
scenes.summary: The taxi drives through countryside, passing a road marker for Krasnystaw.

scenes.heading: INT. TAXI - DAY
scenes.summary: The boys sit in the back of the taxi traveling to their grandmother's town.

scenes.heading: EXT. KRASNYSTAW TOWN SQUARE - DAY
scenes.summary: The taxi arrives at Krasnystaw's quiet town square and the cousins get out with their bags.

scenes.heading: EXT. TOWN SQUARE – DAY
scenes.summary: David and Benji walk across the town square carrying luggage, searching for address number 25.

scenes.heading: EXT. SIDE STREET NEAR CHURCH - DAY
scenes.summary: David leads Benji around a corner past St. Francis Xavier Church toward an alley.

scenes.heading: EXT. ALLEY IN FRONT OF HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: The cousins find their grandmother's unremarkable childhood home at address 25, and Benji shares a story about how their grandmother once slapped him for being late and high to dinner, calling it the best thing that ever happened to him.
scenes.turningPointNote: Finding their grandmother's childhood home and Benji's revelation about being slapped provides emotional catharsis and deeper understanding of their grandmother's love

scenes.heading: EXT. KRASNYTAW TOWN SQUARE - DAY
scenes.summary: David and Benji walk through the town square with their bags and exit as the town continues without them.

scenes.heading: I/E. TAXI - NEAR KRASNYSTAW - DAY
scenes.summary: The cousins sit in a taxi traveling back to Warsaw Airport.

scenes.heading: I/E. TAXI - WARSAW
scenes.summary: The taxi approaches Warsaw Chopin Airport and turns off the exit.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW AIRPORT - DAY
scenes.summary: David and Benji walk through the airport not speaking but comfortable in each other's presence.

scenes.heading: INT. AIRPLANE - DAY
scenes.summary: The cousins sit awake on the airplane, each lost in his own thoughts.

scenes.heading: INT. JFK GATE – DAY
scenes.summary: David and Benji exit the gate at JFK and walk through the airport with other passengers.

scenes.heading: INT. JFK BAGGAGE AREA - DAY
scenes.summary: David invites Benji to dinner with his family, but Benji declines and chooses to stay at the airport alone, saying he likes meeting people there.
scenes.turningPointNote: Benji's decision to stay at the airport rather than connect with David's family represents the final separation and his choice of isolation

scenes.heading: INT. CAB - DAY
scenes.summary: David sits alone in a cab riding back home, looking out the window.

scenes.heading: INT. CAB - DAY
scenes.summary: David's cab passes LeFrak City and Machpelah cemetery as he takes in the Jewish history around him.

scenes.heading: EXT. BROOKLYN STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: David walks down his Brooklyn street, stops in front of his building, and places a stone on his stoop.

scenes.heading: INT. APARTMENT - DAY
scenes.summary: David enters his apartment, calls out to his family, and is embraced by his wife Priya and son Abe as his life seems nice.

scenes.heading: INT. JFK AIRPORT - DAY
scenes.summary: Back at JFK, Benji sits alone on the same airport bench where the film began, watching strangers pass by as the camera zooms in on his face.
scenes.turningPointNote: The circular ending with Benji back on the airport bench reinforces his isolation and completes the film's emotional arc
